problem: A 1.00-g sample of which compound will produce the greatest amount of carbon dioxide after complete combustion with excess oxygen?
Rhenium (Re) consists of two stable isotopes, 185Re and 187Re. The average atomic mass of Re is 186.2 amu. What is the mole percent of 185Re in rhenium?
A 10.00 g sample of a soluble barium salt is treated with an excess of sodium sulfate to precipitate 8.93 g BaSO4 (M = 233.4). Which barium salt is it?
How much Sr(OH)2 * 8 H2O (M = 265.76) is needed to prepare 150.0 mL of solution in which [OH-] = 0.100 M?
Which procedure produces a 0.2 M solution of Na2SO4?
choices: Mixing 500 mL of 0.4 M NaOH with 500 mL of 0.2 M Na2SO4
Mixing 300 mL of 1.2 M NaOH with 600 mL of 0.3 M H2SO4
Dissolving 0.2 mol solid Na2SO4 in 500 mL water
Diluting 400 mL of 0.3 M Na2SO4 to a final volume of 1.0 L

problem: 10.0 g of an alkali metal chloride salt is dissolved in 90.0 g H2O. This solution has a vapor pressure that is 3.2% lower than that of pure water at the same temperature. What is the salt?
What is the mobile phase in thin layer chromatography?
choices: A liquid solvent such as isopropanol
A smooth solid surface such as a glass plate
A coating on a solid surface such as silica gel
A chamber such as a glass jar

problem: Which is the strongest acid?
Which compound, when dissolved in water, will increase the electrical conductivity of water the least?
Which metal reacts most vigorously with water?

A student determines the acetic acid concentration of a sample of distilled vinegar by measuring 25.00 mL of the sample with a volumetric pipet, then titrating the sample with standardized sodium hydroxide solution using phenolphthalein as an indicator. Which error will give an acetic acid content for the vinegar that is too high?
choices: The titration flask has some distilled water in it when the acetic acid sample is added to it.
The volumetric pipet has some distilled water in it when the acetic acid sample is measured with it.
Some of the vinegar remains in the volumetric pipet rather than being dispensed into the titration flask.
The endpoint is recorded when the solution turns dark red instead of faint pink.

problem: Which 50-mL container would be most suitable for measuring and dispensing 37 mL of an aqueous solution? [Images show (A) Erlenmeyer flask, (B) beaker, (C) graduated cylinder, (D) volumetric flask]
What is the partial pressure of Ar in a gaseous mixture of 3.25 g Ar and 2.28 g Kr in a 5.00 L container at 15.0 °C?

Which isomer of C4H10O2 has the highest normal boiling point? [Images show (A) HO-CH2CH2CH2-OH (1,3-propanediol with extra CH2), (B) CH3-O-CH2-O-CH3, (C) CH3-O-CH2CH2-O-CH3 (but with different connectivity), (D) CH3CH(OH)CH(OH)CH3... actually structures shown as drawings]

Which statements about the behavior of gaseous H2 molecules in a container at 1 atm and 298 K are correct?

I. All H2 molecules are moving at the same speed.
II. The H2 molecules are colliding more frequently with the walls of the container than they would in the same container at 398 K.

A portion of the phase diagram of uranium hexafluoride (UF6) is shown below. [Phase diagram showing solid-liquid-gas regions with triple point near 50°C]

Which statements are correct?
I. UF6 sublimes, rather than melts, at 1 atm pressure.
II. At 2 atm and 45 °C, only solid UF6 is present at equilibrium.
How many nearest neighbors does each silicon atom have in a crystal of Si?

A sample of methanol, CH3OH, is introduced into an evacuated chamber with a movable piston. The pressure is measured as a function of the volume of the container while the temperature is maintained at 50 °C, and the graph below is obtained. [Graph shows pressure vs volume with a flat region around 350 mmHg between ~20-60 mL, then decreasing] Which statement best describes this system?
choices: At volumes below 60 mL, methanol is only present in the liquid phase.
At volumes above 60 mL, methanol is only present in the gaseous phase.
If the process were repeated at 60 °C, the point at which the pressure begins to decrease would shift to higher volume.
If more methanol were added to the container, the pressure at V = 20 mL would increase.

problem: The amount of energy released by a chemical process is always equal to that absorbed by the system and surroundings. This is a consequence of which law?

What is DeltaG°f of NO(g) at 298 K?

Substance | DeltaH°f, kJ mol^-1 | S°, J mol^-1 K^-1
N2(g) | 0 | 191.6
O2(g) | 0 | 205.2
NO(g) | 90.3 | 210.8

Which statements about entropy, enthalpy, and energy are correct for the reaction below?

C5H12(g) + 8 O2(g) -> 5 CO2(g) + 6 H2O(g)

I. DeltaS° > 0
II. DeltaH° > DeltaE°

4.00 mL of 2.00 M aqueous NaOH is added to 100.0 mL of 0.200 M aqueous NaHSO4, all initially at 25.00 °C, in a well-insulated flask. Neglecting the effects of dilution, and assuming that volumes are additive and that all solutions have the same density and specific heat capacity as pure water, what is the final temperature of the mixture?

Species | DeltaH°f, kJ mol^-1
OH-(aq) | -230
HSO4-(aq) | -886
SO4^2-(aq) | -909
H2O(l) | -286

The Ksp of Al(OH)3 is 2.0 x 10^-31 at 298 K. What is DeltaG° (at 298 K) for the precipitation of Al(OH)3 according to the equation below?

Al3+(aq) + 3 OH-(aq) -> Al(OH)3(s)

The vapor pressure of solid I2 is measured (in bar) over the temperature range 0-55 °C. The natural logarithms of these vapor pressures are plotted as a function of the reciprocal of the absolute temperature as shown below. [Graph shows ln(vapor pressure) vs 1/T with equation y = 17.44 - 7527x]

What is the standard enthalpy of sublimation (DeltaH°subl) of I2(s)?

Tert-butyl chloride reacts with hydroxide ion in a process that is first order in both tert-butyl chloride and hydroxide. If both reactants are doubled in concentration, how does the reaction rate change?
choices: The effect on the rate cannot be determined from the information given.

problem: Under certain conditions, the rate of decomposition of hydrogen peroxide is first order in H2O2. It is observed that the concentration of H2O2 decreases by 1.00% in 300.0 s. What is the rate constant for the reaction?

The pain reliever naproxen has a half-life of 12 h in the human body. A patient takes a 400 mg tablet of naproxen at 8 AM and a second 400 mg tablet at 8 PM (12 h after the first dose). How much naproxen remains in the patient's body at 8 AM the following day (12 h after the second dose)?

For the aqueous reaction

I2 + HPO(OH)2 + H2O -> PO(OH)3 + 2 I- + 2 H+

the following mechanism is proposed:

HPO(OH)2 <=> P(OH)3    fast, unfavorable
P(OH)3 + I2 -> IP(OH)3+ + I-    slow, irreversible
IP(OH)3+ + H2O -> PO(OH)3 + I- + 2 H+    fast

Which observation is consistent with this mechanism?
choices: The reaction is first order in both HPO(OH)2 and I2.
The reaction rate is independent of the I2 concentration.
The reaction goes faster as the concentration of H+ is increased.
The reaction goes slower as the concentration of I- is increased.

problem:
In which ways may a catalyst increase the rate of a reaction?

I. It may alter the rate law of the reaction.
II. It may make the overall reaction more exothermic.
The rate constant of a chemical reaction increases by 63% when the temperature is raised from 40 °C to 45 °C. What is the activation energy of this reaction?
What mass of lead(II) fluoride (Ksp = 4.0 x 10^-8) will dissolve in 1.00 L of water?
The Ka of the pyridinium ion, C5H5NH+, is 5.9 x 10^-6. What is the pH of a 0.170 M solution of pyridine, C5H5N?
Excess solid silver chloride is added to water and the mixture stirred until equilibrium is achieved. Addition of which substance will increase the concentration of chloride ion in this solution?

Problems 34 and 35 are about the exothermic reaction of iodine with iodide ion shown below.

I2(aq) + I-(aq) <=> I3-(aq)    Keq = 750 (at 25 °C)

1.0 x 10^-4 mol I2 and 4.0 x 10^-3 mol KI are dissolved in water at 25 °C to give 100.0 mL of solution. What is the equilibrium concentration of I2 in this solution?

Which changes will result in an increase in the number of moles of I2(aq) present at equilibrium?

I2(aq) + I-(aq) <=> I3-(aq)    Keq = 750 (at 25 °C), exothermic

I. Increasing the temperature
II. Replacing the KI with an equal mass of NaI

A 0.100 M aqueous solution of H2SeO3 is titrated with 1.000 M NaOH solution. At the point marked with a circle on the titration curve, which species comprise at least 10% of the total selenium in solution? [Graph shows pH vs Volume NaOH with circle at approximately pH 10, volume ~20 mL near second equivalence point]
What is the average oxidation state of iron in riebeckite, Na2Fe5Si8O22(OH)2?
An electrolytic cell is operated for 3000 s using a current of 1.50 A. From which 1.0 M solution will the smallest mass of metal be deposited?

A galvanic cell using an unknown metal M is arranged as shown below. [Diagram shows two half-cells: Half-cell A with 1.00 M M^n+(aq) and Half-cell B with 0.001 M M^n+(aq), connected by a salt bridge] What can be concluded about this cell?
choices: No current flows in the cell since the same metal is used in both half-cells.
Which cell is the cathode depends on the standard reduction potential of M^2+(aq).

problem:
When manganese metal is placed in a solution containing silver(I) ion in acidic solution, metallic silver deposits on the manganese. Which half-reactions are taking place?

I. Ag+(aq) + e- -> Ag(s)
II. Mn(s) + 4 H2O(l) -> MnO4-(aq) + 8 H+(aq) + 7e-

What is E° for the following redox reaction?

5 Te(s) + 4 MnO4-(aq) + 12 H+(aq) -> 5 TeO2(s) + 4 Mn2+(aq) + 6 H2O(l)

Half-reaction | E°, V
TeO2(s) + 4 H+(aq) + 4e- -> Te(s) + 2 H2O(l) | +0.59
MnO4-(aq) + 8 H+(aq) + 5e- -> Mn2+(aq) + 4 H2O | +1.51

The reduction of O2 to H2O in acidic solution has a standard reduction potential of +1.23 V. What is the effect on the half-cell potential at 25 °C when the pH of the solution is decreased by one unit?

O2(g) + 4 H+(aq) + 4e- -> 2 H2O(l)
choices: The half-cell potential decreases by 236 mV.
The half-cell potential increases by 236 mV.
The half-cell potential decreases by 59 mV.
The half-cell potential increases by 59 mV.

problem: Which statement best describes the amount of energy released by gas-phase oxygen and sulfur atoms upon accepting an electron (electron affinities)?
choices: O releases less energy than S because the smaller size of O causes greater electron-electron repulsion.
O releases less energy than S because O has a lower mass than S.
O releases more energy than S because the electron enters a 2p orbital in O but a 3p orbital in S.
O releases more energy than S because O has a larger effective nuclear charge Zeff than S.

4s    3d

What is the wavelength of a photon whose energy is 334 kJ mol^-1?

Which is NOT a valid resonance structure of N-formylurea?

[N-formylurea structure shown: H2N-C(=O)-NH-C(=O)-H]

(A) shows negative charge on oxygen of C=O nearest to NH2, positive charge on N between the two carbonyls
(B) shows negative charge on oxygen of formyl C=O, positive charge on N between the two carbonyls
(C) shows negative charge on oxygen of C=O nearest to NH2, positive charge on the NH2 nitrogen
(D) shows negative charge on oxygen of formyl C=O, positive charge on the NH nitrogen nearest to formyl
Which molecule is best described as having a lone pair on nitrogen in an sp2-hybridized orbital?
Nitric oxide, NO, has a smaller first ionization energy than either N atoms or O atoms. Which is the best explanation for this observation?
choices: The electron that is ionized in NO occupies an antibonding orbital.
NO forms a strong bond, which lowers the ionization energy.
NO has a large dipole moment, which increases electron-electron repulsion.
N atoms have a half-filled 2p subshell, while O is very electronegative. Both of these factors give the isolated atoms unusually high ionization energies.

problem: How many stereoisomers does the octahedral complex Co(NH3)3(CN)3 have?
How many stable isomers have the formula C2H2ClBr?
Which best describes the relationship between the two structures shown below? [Two chair conformations of methylcyclohexane are shown - one with CH3 axial and one with CH3 equatorial]
choices: They are in rapid equilibrium at room temperature and are present in equal quantities.
They are in rapid equilibrium at room temperature and are present in unequal quantities.
